blocks.content: Select the tutorial data source and build a query that monitors request duration for the Quick Pizza application.

blocks.title: Select data source and build query

blocks.content: Select **grafanacloud-play-prom** as your data source. This is the tutorial Prometheus instance you installed in the previous step.

In the query editor, use the **Metric** dropdown to search for and select `quickpizza_server_http_request_duration_seconds_sum`. This metric tracks how long HTTP requests take in the Quick Pizza application.
Leave the **Label filters** empty. You want to watch every Quick Pizza service, so you don't narrow the query to a single service here. Instead, you'll group by service in a later step.
Click **+ Operations** and choose **Range > Rate**, then keep `$__rate_interval`. Rate turns the ever-growing counter into a per-second value so you see current behavior.

Click **+ Operations** again and choose **Aggregations > Sum**. In the **By label** field on the Sum operation, add `job`. This gives you one line per service, so when the alert fires you know which service is slow.
Click **Preview** to verify your query returns data. You should see one line per Quick Pizza service in the preview panel.
Your query selects the data to monitor. Next, you'll set the condition that determines when the alert fires.
